items.title: 0.1.0 — the founding
items.note: Contract, runtime, gate, ratchet, scaffolds, Pit Board, AGENTS.md-first docs. Steerability lab #1 passed with zero JS.

items.title: 0.2.0 — the constellation
items.note: Satellites (explicit capture and honest component manifests), curvature pour, and the first Auth skeleton. Later hardening removed manifest fields and stores that the runtime did not truly enforce.

items.title: Chart + atlas — the third render head
items.note: Agents read coordinates, not pixels. The atlas is a screen whose chart is the atlas. ANOM-170: purposes are law.

items.title: Retired: Concierge v1
items.note: Removed during production hardening. Resident-agent policy is a real product and trust-boundary decision, not an almost-free framework satellite. Chart/Atlas remains the lower-level substrate.

items.title: The website-manifold
items.note: Recast by the owner: the board came home to the demo as the timing tower; the public site becomes its own repo — the stranger test.

items.title: Retired: Concierge model resolver
items.note: Removed with Concierge. Model billing, authorization, intent resolution, and confirmation policy need a product boundary of their own.

items.title: Personal access tokens for visiting agents
items.note: Minted via a form, shown exactly once, stored hashed, presented as Bearer; revocable. Bearer writes skip C-203 by design — no cookie, no CSRF shape.

items.title: Auth: direct OIDC + TOTP
items.note: Hardened: explicit secure config, Origin/Referer protection for every browser write, expiring shared challenges and tokens, bounded TOTP attempts with replay prevention, OIDC issuer/subject identity, PKCE, nonce, mix-up defenses, and no email auto-link. Exercised after pouring into a fresh app.

items.title: Live — server-pushed fragment swaps
items.note: data-live opens the stream; the server pushes fragments; the boost layer swaps by id, skipping missing targets. Proven: curl POSTs, an open browser updates itself, zero reloads.

items.title: Event horizon doctrine (C-7xx)
items.note: The declared boundary where the gate's sight deliberately ends: budgeted, fenced, vendored 60fps enclaves.

items.title: Retired: unfinished Auth stores
items.note: JSON and Mongo adapters were removed during production hardening. SQLite is the proved baseline; another store must demonstrate atomic challenges, replay prevention, shared limits, expiry, and concurrency before it ships.

items.title: ANOM-150/151/160/161
items.note: ANOM-150 orphan CSS (its test replants the lap-board rot and catches it), ANOM-151 registry patterns, ANOM-161 manifest honesty (first catch: Auth's own manifest, missing token_desk), and `curvature audit <pkg>` reaching into installed third-party source per C-801.

items.title: Deployment doc
items.note: docs/DEPLOY.md, written from curvature-site's actual Procfile: one process, four files, two honest proxy notes. No k8s ritual materialized.

items.title: Time trials — honest numbers
items.note: Published whatever they said, and they said: ~2,000 req/s on every head, the full HTML page within 2% of the JSON-API baseline at the same p50 — the SPA saves bytes it later spends client-side. docs/TIME_TRIALS.md; regenerate with scripts/bench.py.

items.title: Retired: framework offline replay
items.note: Removed during production hardening. A generic worker cached authenticated HTML and one-time secrets without application authorization or invalidation knowledge. Offline is now an explicit product architecture, not a framework promise.

items.title: curvature new demo
items.note: Pit Board as a scaffold: only a pour delivers code.

items.title: The design idea (owner's, sealed)
items.note: Requires semantic HTML, which the substrate already speaks. Tailwind remains heresy. Details live in the owner's head.

items.title: Timing tower — the board wears its true clothes
items.note: ON TRACK, SHIPPED with positions, STINT PLAN; paddle forms (OUT/FLAG/PIT); Nano Banana 2 emblem. Poured awake, with an idea in it.

items.title: curvature-site: live stranger test
items.note: Live on Heroku and independently packaged, but currently behind the framework hardening branch. Update from the next release, add deployment headers and health checks, then verify the live HTML and Chart before calling the stranger test complete.

items.title: The gate goes public — CI on both repos
items.note: Same ./gate.sh, witnessed on GitHub. Its first run caught a real bug: CI runners have no git identity, so pour commits now carry the scaffold's own. The badge means what it says.

items.title: v0.2.1 — the bump becomes law (C-403)
items.note: First publish under the standing blessing. ANOM-143 makes stale versions a gate matter; its arrival broke the 300-line ceiling on the gate's own modules — the first ANOM-140 in history forced the bounds-family split while it was cheap.

items.title: 0.2.4 production hardening
items.note: Hardened boost failure semantics and races; immutable Elements; honest Chart fields; history-aware ratchet; pyright and Python 3.12–3.14 CI; fresh-pour Auth security tests; removal of Concierge, offline replay, unenforced manifest fields, and unfinished stores. Evidence: 195 framework tests, 98.5% coverage, a clean 3.12 wheel stranger with poured Auth, and the site gate against the exact candidate. Candidate is not yet published or deployed.
